questions.text: According to the laws of reflection, the angle of incidence is ________ the angle of reflection.
questions.explanation: The first law of reflection states that the angle of incidence is always equal to the angle of reflection.

questions.text: An image formed by a plane mirror is always:
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors form virtual, erect images that are laterally inverted and the same size as the object.

questions.text: The center of the reflecting surface of a spherical mirror is called the:
questions.explanation: The pole (P) is the geometric center of the reflecting surface of a spherical mirror.

questions.text: The relationship between the radius of curvature (R) and focal length (f) of a spherical mirror is:
questions.explanation: For spherical mirrors of small apertures, the radius of curvature is found to be twice the focal length.

questions.text: Which mirror is used by dentists to see large images of teeth?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors produce a magnified virtual image when the object is placed close to the mirror.

questions.text: A mirror that always forms a virtual, erect, and diminished image is a:
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always form diminished, virtual, and erect images regardless of the object's position.

questions.text: The mirror formula is expressed as:
questions.explanation: The mirror formula relates object distance (u), image distance (v), and focal length (f).

questions.text: In the New Cartesian Sign Convention, the object is always placed to the ________ of the mirror.
questions.explanation: By convention, the object is placed to the left so that light falls from left to right.

questions.text: Linear magnification (m) produced by a spherical mirror is:
questions.explanation: Magnification is the ratio of image height to object height, also given by -v/u for mirrors.

questions.text: If magnification has a minus sign, the image is:
questions.explanation: A negative value for magnification indicates that the image is real and inverted.

questions.text: The bending of light when it passes from one medium to another is called:
questions.explanation: Refraction is the change in direction of light as it enters a new medium obliquely.

questions.text: When light travels from a rarer medium to a denser medium, it bends:
questions.explanation: Light slows down in a denser medium, causing it to bend towards the normal.

questions.text: Snell's law of refraction is expressed as:
questions.explanation: Snell's law states that the ratio of the sine of the angle of incidence to the sine of the angle of refraction is constant.

questions.text: The refractive index of a medium with respect to vacuum is called its:
questions.explanation: Absolute refractive index (n) = Speed of light in vacuum (c) / Speed of light in medium (v).

questions.text: A ray of light traveling from air enters a glass slab. Compared to air, the speed of light in glass will be:
questions.explanation: Glass is optically denser than air, so the speed of light decreases in glass.

questions.text: A lens that is thicker at the middle than at the edges is a:
questions.explanation: Convex lenses are converging lenses that are thicker in the center.

questions.text: The power of a lens is defined as the reciprocal of its:
questions.explanation: Power (P) = 1/f, where focal length f must be in meters.

questions.text: The SI unit of power of a lens is:
questions.explanation: One dioptre (D) is the power of a lens whose focal length is 1 meter.

questions.text: The focal length of a convex lens is:
questions.explanation: By sign convention, the focal length of a converging (convex) lens is positive.

questions.text: The Lens Formula is given by:
questions.explanation: The lens formula relates u, v, and f, distinguishing it from the mirror formula by the minus sign.

questions.text: Where should an object be placed in front of a convex lens to get a real image of the same size?
questions.explanation: When an object is at 2F, the image is formed at 2F on the other side, and it is real and same-sized.

questions.text: A concave lens always produces an image that is:
questions.explanation: Concave (diverging) lenses always form virtual, erect, and diminished images.

questions.text: The magnification (m) produced by a lens is related to object and image distance by:
questions.explanation: For lenses, magnification is positive v/u, unlike mirrors where it is negative v/u.

questions.text: If the power of a lens is -2.0 D, its focal length is:
The focal length of a plane mirror is:
questions.explanation: A plane mirror is considered a spherical mirror with an infinite radius of curvature, thus f is infinity.

questions.text: No matter how far you stand from a mirror, your image appears erect. The mirror is likely to be:
questions.explanation: Both plane and convex mirrors always produce erect images of objects placed in front of them.

questions.text: Which of the following materials cannot be used to make a lens?
questions.explanation: Lenses must be made of transparent materials that can refract light; clay is opaque.

questions.text: The path of a ray of light coming from air passing through a rectangular glass slab is traced by four students. The correct one shows:
options: Bending away from normal at first surface
Bending towards normal at first surface
Bending towards normal at second surface
questions.explanation: Light entering glass (denser) from air (rarer) always bends towards the normal.

questions.text: The effective diameter of the reflecting surface of a spherical mirror is called its:
questions.explanation: Aperture refers to the width of the reflecting area of the mirror.

questions.text: A point object is placed at the center of curvature of a concave mirror. The distance of its image from the pole is:
questions.explanation: When the object is at C (2f), the image is also formed at C (2f).

questions.text: The refractive index of Diamond is 2.42. What does this suggest?
options: Speed of light is highest in diamond
Speed of light in diamond is 1/2.42 times that in vacuum
questions.explanation: Higher refractive index means light travels much slower in that medium compared to vacuum.

questions.text: To obtain a magnification of -0.5 with a convex lens, the object should be placed:
questions.explanation: A negative magnification less than 1 indicates a real, diminished image, which occurs when the object is beyond 2F.

questions.text: A ray of light passes through the optical center of a thin lens. It will:
questions.explanation: Light rays passing through the optical center of any lens emerge without any deviation.

questions.text: Two thin lenses of power +3.5 D and -2.5 D are placed in contact. The power of the combination is:
Real images are formed by a ________ mirror.
questions.explanation: Only concave mirrors can form real images (when the object is beyond the focus).

questions.text: When an object is kept at infinity, the image formed by a concave mirror is at:
questions.explanation: Rays from infinity are parallel to the principal axis and converge at the principal focus after reflection.

questions.text: Lateral displacement in a glass slab depends on:
questions.explanation: The shift of the emergent ray depends on the medium's density, width, and how the light enters.

questions.text: A student uses a lens of focal length +50 cm and another of -50 cm. These lenses are respectively:
questions.explanation: Positive focal length indicates a convex lens; negative indicates a concave lens.

questions.text: The image of an object formed by a convex mirror is observed to be virtual, erect and diminished. The object is:
options: At any position in front of it
questions.explanation: A convex mirror always produces virtual, erect, and diminished images for all finite distances.

questions.text: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
questions.explanation: Light travels at its maximum speed of 300,000 km/s or 3 x 10^8 m/s in vacuum.
